**Soundless voices, silenced selves: are auditory verbal hallucinations in schizophrenia truly perceptual?**. Lancet Psychiatry.. PMID: 38631367. DOI: 10.1016/S2215-0366(24)00061-0. In much contemporary psychiatric training and practice, there is astrong emphasis on the audible or perceptual quality and externality of auditory verbal hallucinations in clinical assessments. Atypical question during clinical assessment is asking whether the voices that aperson hears sound identical to the way the clinician'svoice is heard.
